    inherit (parsed_lock_data.${name}.${mkExtrasKey {inherit extras;}}) sources;
    inherit (parsed_lock_data.${name}.${mkExtrasKey {inherit extras;}}) version;

          value = parsed_lock_data.${name}.${mkExtrasKey {inherit extras;}};
          value = parsed_lock_data.${dep.name}.${mkExtrasKey dep};
    parsed_lock_data,
        value = mkDepEntry parsed_lock_data dep;

Match found in: ./09/26/synapse-system/vendor/nix/dream2nix/modules/dream2nix/rust-cargo-lock/translate.nix
  parseSpdxId,
      # Filter root referencing member, we already parsed this (rootToml)
    # Parse Cargo.lock and extract dependencies
    parsedLock = projectTree.files."Cargo.lock".tomlContent;
    parsedDeps = parsedLock.package;
    getChecksum = dep: parsedLock.metadata."checksum ${makeDepId dep}";
      parsedDeps;
    # takes a parsed dependency entry and finds the original dependency from `Cargo.lock`
    # This parses a "package-name version (source)" entry in the "dependencies"
    parseDepEntryImpl = entry: let
      parsed = l.splitString " " entry;
      name = l.head parsed;
      # parse the version if it exists
        if l.length parsed > 1
        then l.elemAt parsed 1
      # parse the source if it exists
        if l.length parsed > 2
        then l.removePrefix "(" (l.removeSuffix ")" (l.elemAt parsed 2))
      makePair = entry: l.nameValuePair entry (parseDepEntryImpl entry);
      depEntries = l.flatten (l.map (dep: dep.dependencies or []) parsedDeps);
    parseDepEntry = entry: entryToDependencyAttrs.${entry};
    # Parses a git source, taken straight from nixpkgs.
    parseSourceImpl = src: let
    parsedSources = l.listToAttrs (
      (dep: l.nameValuePair dep.source (parseSourceImpl dep.source))
      (l.filter (dep: dep ? source) parsedDeps)
    parseSource = dep:
      then parsedSources.${dep.source}
      source = parseSource rawObj;
            (l.map parseSource parsedDeps)
                {license = parseSpdxId licenseRaw;}
      serializedRawObjects = parsedDeps;
          (l.map parseDepEntry (rawObj.dependencies or []));
          source = parseSource rawObj;
              parsed = source.value;
                if parsed.type or null == "branch"
                then {ref = "refs/heads/${parsed.value}";}
                else if parsed.type or null == "tag"
                then {ref = "refs/tags/${parsed.value}";}
                inherit (parsed) url;
                rev = parsed.sha;

Match found in: ./09/26/synapse-system/vendor/nix/uv2nix/lib/lock1.nix
  inherit (pyproject-nix.lib.pep508) parseMarkers evalMarkers;
  inherit (pyproject-nix.lib.pypa) parseWheelFileName;
      # Lock file as parsed by parseLock
              evalMarkers environ (parseMarkers markers)
    Parse unmarshaled uv.lock
  parseLock =
      parseOptions =
      # This is so that we only have to parse a marker exactly once even if the marker is repeated
        map (markers: nameValuePair markers (parseMarkers markers)) (
      requires-python = pep440.parseVersionConds requires-python;
      manifest = self.parseManifest manifest;
      package = map (self.parsePackage topLevelMarkers) package;
      options = parseOptions options;
  parseManifest =
    Parse a package entry from uv.lock
  parsePackage =
      parseWheel =
          file' = parseWheelFileName (baseNameOf url);
          file' = parseWheelFileName filename;
          file' = parseWheelFileName (baseNameOf path);
      parseMetadata =
          parseRequires =
              marker = if marker != null then parseMarkers marker else null;
              specifier = if specifier != null then pep440.parseVersionConds specifier else null;
          requires-dist = map parseRequires requires-dist;
          requires-dev = mapAttrs (_: map parseRequires) requires-dev;
      # Parse marker, but avoid parsing markers already present in toplevel uv.lock marker fields
      parseMarker = marker: topLevelMarkers.${marker} or (parseMarkers marker);
      inherit (pep440) parseVersion;
      parseDependency =
          version' = if version != null then parseVersion version else null;
          marker = if marker != null then parseMarker marker else null;
      version' = pep440.parseVersion version;
      wheels = map parseWheel wheels;
      metadata = parseMetadata metadata;
      # Don't parse resolution-markers.
      dependencies = map parseDependency dependencies;
      optional-dependencies = mapAttrs (_: map parseDependency) optional-dependencies;
      dev-dependencies = mapAttrs (_: map parseDependency) dev-dependencies;
                requires-python = null; # requires-python as parsed by pep621.parseRequiresPython

Match found in: ./10/10/nix2/qa/test-prelude.nix
  # Test 1: Ensure parsedUrls is not empty
  testParsedUrlsNotEmpty = lib.assertMsg (lib.length prelude > 0) "Parsed URLs list should not be empty.";
  # Test 2: Ensure each parsed URL has expected components
  testParsedUrlComponents = lib.map (parsedUrl:
    lib.assertMsg (parsedUrl ? owner && parsedUrl ? repo && parsedUrl ? url)
      "Each parsed URL should have 'owner', 'repo', and 'url' components."
  testOwnerIsAllowed = lib.map (parsedUrl:
    lib.assertMsg (parsedUrl.owner == "meta-introspector")
      externalRepos = lib.filter (parsedUrl: parsedUrl.owner != "meta-introspector") prelude;
      metaIntrospectorRepos = lib.filter (parsedUrl: parsedUrl.owner == "meta-introspector") prelude;
      metaIntrospectorRepos = lib.filter (parsedUrl: parsedUrl.owner == "meta-introspector") prelude;
    inherit testParsedUrlsNotEmpty testParsedUrlComponents testOwnerIsAllowed testExternalReposHaveMetaIntrospectorForks testMetaIntrospectorForksAreCheckedOutAndBranched;
  allTestsPass = testParsedUrlsNotEmpty && (lib.all (x: x) testParsedUrlComponents) && (lib.all (x: x) testOwnerIsAllowed) && testExternalReposHaveMetaIntrospectorForks && testMetaIntrospectorForksAreCheckedOutAndBranched;
